    {"repo": "sg-evals/data-plane-api--84e84367", "path": "envoy/service/route/v3/rds.proto", "symbol": "VirtualHostDiscoveryService"},
    {"repo": "sg-evals/data-plane-api--84e84367", "path": "envoy/service/route/v3/srds.proto", "symbol": "ScopedRoutesDiscoveryService"},
    {"repo": "sg-evals/data-plane-api--84e84367", "path": "envoy/service/runtime/v3/rtds.proto", "symbol": "RuntimeDiscoveryService"},
    {"repo": "sg-evals/data-plane-api--84e84367", "path": "envoy/service/secret/v3/sds.proto", "symbol": "SecretDiscoveryService"},
    {"repo": "sg-evals/data-plane-api--84e84367", "path": "envoy/service/status/v3/csds.proto", "symbol": "ClientStatusDiscoveryService"},
    {"repo": "sg-evals/data-plane-api--84e84367", "path": "envoy/service/tap/v2alpha/tap.proto", "symbol": "TapSinkService"},
    {"repo": "sg-evals/grpc--957dba5e", "path": "src/proto/grpc/health/v1/health.proto", "symbol": "Health"}
  ],
  "text": "## Cross-Organization gRPC Service Definitions\n\nThis analysis identifies the gRPC service definitions shared between Envoy and gRPC projects, covering both xDS (Envoy) services and the standard gRPC Health Check service.\n\n### Envoy xDS Service Definitions (envoyproxy/data-plane-api)\n\nThe data-plane-api repository contains 31 proto files defining gRPC services across multiple versions (v2, v2alpha, v3). These include:\n\n**Core xDS Discovery Services:**\n- **AggregatedDiscoveryService** (envoy/service/discovery/v2/ads.proto, envoy/service/discovery/v3/ads.proto): Multiplexed discovery for all resource types\n- **ClusterDiscoveryService** (envoy/service/cluster/v3/cds.proto): CDS for cluster configuration\n- **EndpointDiscoveryService** (envoy/service/endpoint/v3/eds.proto): EDS for endpoint discovery\n- **ListenerDiscoveryService** (envoy/service/listener/v3/lds.proto): LDS for listener configuration\n- **RouteDiscoveryService** & **VirtualHostDiscoveryService** (envoy/service/route/v3/rds.proto): RDS for route configuration\n- **ScopedRoutesDiscoveryService** (envoy/service/route/v3/srds.proto): SRDS for scoped route configuration\n- **SecretDiscoveryService** (envoy/service/discovery/v2/sds.proto, envoy/service/secret/v3/sds.proto): SDS for secret management\n\n**Extended Services:**\n- **AccessLogService** (envoy/service/accesslog/v2/als.proto, envoy/service/accesslog/v3/als.proto): For streaming access logs\n- **Authorization** (envoy/service/auth/v2/external_auth.proto, envoy/service/auth/v3/external_auth.proto): External authorization service\n- **EventReportingService** (envoy/service/event_reporting/v3/event_reporting_service.proto): Event streaming\n- **ExternalProcessor** (envoy/service/ext_proc/v3/external_processor.proto): HTTP external processing\n- **ExtensionConfigDiscoveryService** (envoy/service/extension/v3/config_discovery.proto): ECDS for filter configuration\n- **HealthDiscoveryService** (envoy/service/discovery/v2/hds.proto, envoy/service/health/v3/hds.proto): Health status reporting\n- **LoadReportingService** (envoy/service/load_stats/v3/lrs.proto): LRS for load reporting\n- **MetricsService** (envoy/service/metrics/v2/metrics_service.proto, envoy/service/metrics/v3/metrics_service.proto): Metrics streaming\n- **NetworkExternalProcessor** (envoy/service/network_ext_proc/v3/network_external_processor.proto): Network-level external processing\n- **RateLimitService** (envoy/service/ratelimit/v2/rls.proto, envoy/service/ratelimit/v3/rls.proto): Global rate limiting\n- **RateLimitQuotaService** (envoy/service/rate_limit_quota/v3/rlqs.proto): Rate limit quota management\n- **RedisProxyExternalAuth** (envoy/service/redis_auth/v3/redis_external_auth.proto): Redis external authentication\n- **RuntimeDiscoveryService** (envoy/service/discovery/v2/rtds.proto, envoy/service/runtime/v3/rtds.proto): RTDS for runtime configuration\n- **ClientStatusDiscoveryService** (envoy/service/status/v3/csds.proto): CSDS for client status\n- **TapSinkService** (envoy/service/tap/v2alpha/tap.proto): For tap sink streaming\n\n### gRPC Health Check Service (grpc/grpc)\n\nThe gRPC repository implements the standard `grpc_health_v1.Health` service across multiple languages:\n\n**Proto Definition:**\n- src/proto/grpc/health/v1/health.proto: Core Health service definition with Check and Watch RPC methods\n\n**C++ Implementation:**\n- src/cpp/server/health/health_check_service.cc: Base health check service implementation\n- src/cpp/server/health/default_health_check_service.cc: Default implementation with actual check logic\n- src/cpp/server/health/health_check_service_server_builder_option.cc: Server builder integration\n\n**Python Implementation:**\n- src/python/grpcio_health_checking/grpc_health/v1/health.py: Synchronous health servicer\n- src/python/grpcio_health_checking/grpc_health/v1/_async.py: Asynchronous health servicer\n\n**Testing:**\n- test/cpp/end2end/test_health_check_service_impl.cc: Comprehensive test implementation\n\n### Key Differences\n\nThe Envoy xDS services are domain-specific discovery and control plane protocols designed for proxy configuration management, while the gRPC Health service is a universal health-checking mechanism defined in grpc-proto and implemented across all major gRPC language implementations. The Health service enables liveness detection at the gRPC protocol level, whereas xDS services handle higher-level infrastructure configuration."
}

- `github.com/sg-evals/data-plane-api--84e84367` — use `repo:^github.com/sg-evals/data-plane-api--84e84367$` filter
- `github.com/sg-evals/envoy--v1.31.2` — use `repo:^github.com/sg-evals/envoy--v1.31.2$` filter
- `github.com/sg-evals/go-control-plane--71637ad6` — use `repo:^github.com/sg-evals/go-control-plane--71637ad6$` filter
- `github.com/sg-evals/grpc--957dba5e` — use `repo:^github.com/sg-evals/grpc--957dba5e$` filter

Scope ALL keyword_search/nls_search queries to these repos.
Use the repo name as the `repo` parameter for read_file/go_to_definition/find_references.


## Required Workflow

1. **Search first** — Use MCP tools to find relevant files and understand existing patterns
2. **Read remotely** — Use `sg_read_file` to read full file contents from Sourcegraph
3. **Edit locally** — Use Edit, Write, and Bash to create or modify files in your working directory
4. **Verify locally** — Run tests with Bash to check your changes

## Tool Selection

| Goal | Tool |
|------|------|
| Exact symbol/string | `sg_keyword_search` |
| Concepts/semantic search | `sg_nls_search` |
| Trace usage/callers | `sg_find_references` |
| See implementation | `sg_go_to_definition` |
| Read full file | `sg_read_file` |
| Browse structure | `sg_list_files` |
| Find repos | `sg_list_repos` |
| Search commits | `sg_commit_search` |
| Track changes | `sg_diff_search` |
| Compare versions | `sg_compare_revisions` |

**Decision logic:**
1. Know the exact symbol? → `sg_keyword_search`
2. Know the concept, not the name? → `sg_nls_search`
3. Need definition of a symbol? → `sg_go_to_definition`
4. Need all callers/references? → `sg_find_references`
5. Need full file content? → `sg_read_file`

## Scoping (Always Do This)

```
repo:^github.com/ORG/REPO$           # Exact repo (preferred)
repo:github.com/ORG/                 # All repos in org
file:.*\.ts$                         # TypeScript only
file:src/api/                        # Specific directory
```

Start narrow. Expand only if results are empty.

## Efficiency Rules

- Chain searches logically: search → read → references → definition
- Don't re-search for the same pattern; use results from prior calls
- Prefer `sg_keyword_search` over `sg_nls_search` when you have exact terms
- Read 2-3 related files before synthesising, rather than one at a time
- Don't read 20+ remote files without writing code — once you understand the pattern, start implementing

## If Stuck

If MCP search returns no results:
1. Broaden the search query (synonyms, partial identifiers)
2. Try `sg_nls_search` for semantic matching
3. Use `sg_list_files` to browse the directory structure
4. Use `sg_list_repos` to verify the repository name

# Cross-Org gRPC Service Definition Discovery

## Your Task

Find the gRPC service definitions that are shared between `envoyproxy/envoy` and `grpc/grpc`. Specifically: 1. In `envoyproxy/data-plane-api`, find all `.proto` files that define `service` declarations using `rpc` methods (the xDS service definitions). 2. In `grpc/grpc`, find the source files that implement the `grpc_health_v1.Health` service (the standard gRPC health checking protocol). Report the repo, file path, and service name for each.

## Context

You are working on a codebase task involving repos from the crossorg domain.

## Available Resources

The local `/workspace/` directory contains: sg-evals/envoy--v1.31.2, sg-evals/data-plane-api--84e84367, sg-evals/go-control-plane--71637ad6, sg-evals/grpc--957dba5e.

**Note:** Additional repositories are accessible via Sourcegraph MCP tools:
- `sg-evals/envoy--v1.31.2` (envoyproxy/envoy)
- `sg-evals/data-plane-api--84e84367` (envoyproxy/data-plane-api)
- `sg-evals/go-control-plane--71637ad6` (envoyproxy/go-control-plane)
- `sg-evals/grpc--957dba5e` (grpc/grpc)

## Output Format

Create a file at `/workspace/answer.json` with your findings in the following structure:

```json
{
  "files": [
    {"repo": "org/repo-name", "path": "relative/path/to/file.go"}
  ],
  "symbols": [
    {"repo": "org/repo-name", "path": "relative/path/to/file.go", "symbol": "SymbolName"}
  ],
  "chain": [
    {"repo": "org/repo-name", "path": "relative/path/to/file.go", "symbol": "FunctionName"}
  ],
  "text": "Narrative explanation of your findings, citing repos and file paths."
}
```

Include only the fields relevant to this task. Your answer is evaluated against a closed-world oracle — completeness matters.

## Evaluation

Your answer will be scored on:
- **File recall and precision**: Did you find all relevant files?
I'll help you find the gRPC service definitions across these repositories. Let me start by exploring both repositories systematically.
Now let me compile the complete list of all service definitions. Let me search more comprehensively for all remaining service directories.
Now let me also check the remaining v2 versions and any other proto files I might have missed. Let me also check the v2/v3 versions of the accesslog, auth, and ratelimit services.
Now let me explore the grpc repository for the Health service implementation files. Let me check the structure:
Now I'll compile my findings into the answer file:

Perfect! I've successfully completed the analysis and created the answer file. Here's a summary of what I found:

## Summary

I've identified **31 gRPC service definitions** in the Envoy data-plane-api repository and the **gRPC Health service** implementation across multiple language bindings in the gRPC repository.

### Key Findings:

**Envoy xDS Service Definitions (31 files across v2, v2alpha, and v3):**
- **Core Discovery Services**: ADS, CDS, EDS, LDS, RDS, SDS, RTDS, LEDS, SRDS
- **Extended Services**: AccessLogService, Authorization, EventReportingService, ExternalProcessor, ExtensionConfigDiscoveryService, HealthDiscoveryService, LoadReportingService, MetricsService, NetworkExternalProcessor, RateLimitService, RateLimitQuotaService, RedisProxyExternalAuth, ClientStatusDiscoveryService, TapSinkService

**gRPC Health Service:**
- Proto definition: `src/proto/grpc/health/v1/health.proto` (Health service with Check and Watch methods)
- C++ implementations: default_health_check_service.cc, health_check_service.cc
- Python implementations: health.py (sync), _async.py (async)

The answer has been written to `/workspace/answer.json` with complete file paths, service symbols, and a detailed narrative explanation of the findings.

  "text": "## Cross-Organization gRPC Service Definitions\n\nThis analysis identifies the gRPC service definitions shared between Envoy and gRPC projects, covering both xDS (Envoy) services and the standard gRPC Health Check service.\n\n### Envoy xDS Service Definitions (envoyproxy/data-plane-api)\n\nThe data-plane-api repository contains 31 proto files defining gRPC services across multiple versions (v2, v2alpha, v3). These include:\n\n**Core xDS Discovery Services:**\n- **AggregatedDiscoveryService** (envoy/service/discovery/v2/ads.proto, envoy/service/discovery/v3/ads.proto): Multiplexed discovery for all resource types\n- **ClusterDiscoveryService** (envoy/service/cluster/v3/cds.proto): CDS for cluster configuration\n- **EndpointDiscoveryService** (envoy/service/endpoint/v3/eds.proto): EDS for endpoint discovery\n- **ListenerDiscoveryService** (envoy/service/listener/v3/lds.proto): LDS for listener configuration\n- **RouteDiscoveryService** & **VirtualHostDiscoveryService** (envoy/service/route/v3/rds.proto): RDS for route configuration\n- **ScopedRoutesDiscoveryService** (envoy/service/route/v3/srds.proto): SRDS for scoped route configuration\n- **SecretDiscoveryService** (envoy/service/discovery/v2/sds.proto, envoy/service/secret/v3/sds.proto): SDS for secret management\n\n**Extended Services:**\n- **AccessLogService** (envoy/service/accesslog/v2/als.proto, envoy/service/accesslog/v3/als.proto): For streaming access logs\n- **Authorization** (envoy/service/auth/v2/external_auth.proto, envoy/service/auth/v3/external_auth.proto): External authorization service\n- **EventReportingService** (envoy/service/event_reporting/v3/event_reporting_service.proto): Event streaming\n- **ExternalProcessor** (envoy/service/ext_proc/v3/external_processor.proto): HTTP external processing\n- **ExtensionConfigDiscoveryService** (envoy/service/extension/v3/config_discovery.proto): ECDS for filter configuration\n- **HealthDiscoveryService** (envoy/service/discovery/v2/hds.proto, envoy/service/health/v3/hds.proto): Health status reporting\n- **LoadReportingService** (envoy/service/load_stats/v3/lrs.proto): LRS for load reporting\n- **MetricsService** (envoy/service/metrics/v2/metrics_service.proto, envoy/service/metrics/v3/metrics_service.proto): Metrics streaming\n- **NetworkExternalProcessor** (envoy/service/network_ext_proc/v3/network_external_processor.proto): Network-level external processing\n- **RateLimitService** (envoy/service/ratelimit/v2/rls.proto, envoy/service/ratelimit/v3/rls.proto): Global rate limiting\n- **RateLimitQuotaService** (envoy/service/rate_limit_quota/v3/rlqs.proto): Rate limit quota management\n- **RedisProxyExternalAuth** (envoy/service/redis_auth/v3/redis_external_auth.proto): Redis external authentication\n- **RuntimeDiscoveryService** (envoy/service/discovery/v2/rtds.proto, envoy/service/runtime/v3/rtds.proto): RTDS for runtime configuration\n- **ClientStatusDiscoveryService** (envoy/service/status/v3/csds.proto): CSDS for client status\n- **TapSinkService** (envoy/service/tap/v2alpha/tap.proto): For tap sink streaming\n\n### gRPC Health Check Service (grpc/grpc)\n\nThe gRPC repository implements the standard `grpc_health_v1.Health` service across multiple languages:\n\n**Proto Definition:**\n- src/proto/grpc/health/v1/health.proto: Core Health service definition with Check and Watch RPC methods\n\n**C++ Implementation:**\n- src/cpp/server/health/health_check_service.cc: Base health check service implementation\n- src/cpp/server/health/default_health_check_service.cc: Default implementation with actual check logic\n- src/cpp/server/health/health_check_service_server_builder_option.cc: Server builder integration\n\n**Python Implementation:**\n- src/python/grpcio_health_checking/grpc_health/v1/health.py: Synchronous health servicer\n- src/python/grpcio_health_checking/grpc_health/v1/_async.py: Asynchronous health servicer\n\n**Testing:**\n- test/cpp/end2end/test_health_check_service_impl.cc: Comprehensive test implementation\n\n### Key Differences\n\nThe Envoy xDS services are domain-specific discovery and control plane protocols designed for proxy configuration management, while the gRPC Health service is a universal health-checking mechanism defined in grpc-proto and implemented across all major gRPC language implementations. The Health service enables liveness detection at the gRPC protocol level, whereas xDS services handle higher-level infrastructure configuration."
}
